title: pypodman port: `-l/--last` is not implemented and "pypodman port $ctn $port" does not work well
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

**kind bug**

**Description**
As the title says. 
* Ones work fine:
1. `pypodman port -a/--all`
2. `pypodman port $ctn`
* Which not work:
1. `pypodman port -l/--last`
2. `pypodman port $ctn $port`

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
On remote:
```
[root@rhel8-1 ~]# podman ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE                             COMMAND      CREATED        STATUS              PORTS                    NAMES
5755956c556f   docker.io/library/fedora:latest   sleep 2000   21 hours ago   Up 10 minutes ago   0.0.0.0:8080->8080/tcp   test-port1
8111ad77c355   docker.io/library/fedora:latest   sleep 2000   21 hours ago   Up 10 minutes ago   0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp       test-port
```
On host:

1.  `~/.local/bin/pypodman -l root --host 192.168.122.213 port -l`

2.  `~/.local/bin/pypodman -l root --host 192.168.122.213 port test-port 80`

3.  `~/.local/bin/pypodman -l root --host 192.168.122.213 port test-port 80/tcp`



**Describe the results you received:**
1. 
```
[root@localhost ~]# ~/.local/bin/pypodman -l root --host 192.168.122.213 port -l
2018-12-11 16:29:39 CST | ERROR    | pypodman: unrecognized arguments: -l
2018-12-11 16:29:39 CST | ERROR    | Try 'pypodman --help' for more information.
```

2. 
```
[root@localhost ~]# ~/.local/bin/pypodman -l root --host 192.168.122.213 port test-port 80
Container "80" not found
8111ad77c3551c8672a9dd7906787e4bf6eef530f9ae6aab134063ff50d623d7
[{'host_port': '80', 'host_ip': '', 'protocol': 'tcp', 'container_port': '80'}]
```

3. 
```
[root@localhost ~]# ~/.local/bin/pypodman -l root --host 192.168.122.213 port test-port 80/tcp
Container "80/tcp" not found
8111ad77c3551c8672a9dd7906787e4bf6eef530f9ae6aab134063ff50d623d7
[{'host_port': '80', 'host_ip': '', 'protocol': 'tcp', 'container_port': '80'}]
```

**Expected result:**
`podman port` results on remote:
1. 
```
[root@rhel8-1 ~]# podman port -l
8080/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:8080
```
2. 
```
[root@rhel8-1 ~]# podman port test-port 80
0.0.0.0:80
```
3.
```
[root@rhel8-1 ~]# podman port test-port 80/tcp
0.0.0.0:80
```

comments.body: We don't want to support --last for remote API, since it opens up the chance of race conditions to too large.

@rhatdan Yes sir, it makes sense.
I think `pypodman port $ctn $port` should be implemented, it seems no other risks included.
